acceptance_criteria: Spreadsheet formulas are inspectable via a 'formulas' sheet or formulas.txt mapping.
An 'inputs' sheet provides row-level citations to the embedded packet for every row used in calculations.
A 'budget' sheet states max_budget and shows arithmetic supporting '43,300 remaining'.
A 'post_remediation' sheet shows numeric pre/post discordance values demonstrating O1 resolves R1.
agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json are present in canonical schema and capture any tool errors.
Deterministic precheck re-run yields parseable artifact previews or attached formula mapping, enabling graders to verify in-sheet formulas and traceability.
Spreadsheet formulas are inspectable via a 'formulas' sheet or formulas.txt mapping.
An 'inputs' sheet provides row-level citations to the embedded packet for every row used in calculations.
A 'budget' sheet states max_budget and shows arithmetic supporting '43,300 remaining'.

planned_changes.description: Make spreadsheet formulas verifiable: add a 'formulas' sheet listing each computed cell (sheet name + cell address), the exact formula text, and the explicit input cell references used. Alternatively, include a plain-text formula mapping file (formulas.txt) in the attempt directory.

Add an 'inputs' sheet in endpoint_adjudication_3.xlsx that contains the original packet values for each row and a clear row-level citation (e.g., packet_row_id or embedded-packet reference) so every spreadsheet row can be traced to the source.
Create a 'budget' sheet that states max_budget, lists option costs, shows arithmetic demonstrating that selecting O1 leaves 43,300 remaining, and documents any assumptions about budget applicability.

Add a 'post_remediation' sheet showing pre- and post-remediation discordance values per record for O1 (numeric columns), plus explicit sensitivity-window comparisons (e.g., discordance_after vs threshold +/- 1) to substantiate the claim that R1 is resolved.

If .xlsx parsing is unreliable in grader environments, also provide machine-readable fallbacks: a CSV of inputs, formulas.txt mapping, and PNG screenshots of key formula cells. Ensure the fallbacks are co-located with the spreadsheet artifact.

Produce canonical agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json in the attempt directory. Ensure trace steps use the exact required field names and include any errors, commands run, and artifact write actions. Capture the codex_cli error previously observed and the remedial steps taken.

Update expert_memo.md to (a) state the explicit max_budget value and reference the 'budget' sheet, (b) reference the 'formulas' and 'post_remediation' sheets when claiming remediation effects, and (c) mark the threshold provenance as needs_review and include directions to rerun sensitivity if the numeric cutoff changes.
Run the deterministic precheck locally and include the parseable previews (or the formula mapping) as attachments so automated graders can verify formula-level traceability.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Spreadsheet formulas and in-sheet traceability could not be programmatically verified (openpyxl unavailable in grader environment). The memo contains assertions (budget remainder, post-remediation discordance) that lack machine-verifiable spreadsheet evidence. The attempt's execution trace also lacks a canonical agent_trace.json/actual_outputs.json. Revise to provide machine- or human-verifiable formula mappings, explicit budget arithmetic, pre/post remediation calculations, and a complete canonical trace so graders can confirm rubric items.
